window_summary: This window tracks the evolution of the httpie/cli command-line HTTP client from early releases through version 3.2.4, highlighting major feature additions, security fixes, and compatibility updates.

chunks.title: v0.2.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: v0.2.1
Tag: 0.2.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2015-02-01T14:07:39Z; created_at: 2012-06-13T14:01:23Z
Release notes:
- Added compatibility with `requests-0.12.1`.
- Dropped custom JSON and HTTP lexers in favor of the ones newly included
in `pygments-1.5`.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release v0.2.1, the project added compatibility with requests-0.12.1 and dropped custom JSON and HTTP lexers in favor of the ones newly included in pygments-1.5.

chunks.title: v0.2.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: v0.2.0
Tag: 0.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2015-02-01T14:08:06Z; created_at: 2012-04-25T00:16:10Z
Release notes:
- Added Python 3 support.
- Added the ability to print the HTTP request as well as the response (see `--print` and `--verbose`).
- Added support for Digest authentication.
- Added file upload support (`http -f POST file_field_name@/path/to/file`).
- Improved syntax highlighting for JSON.
- Added support for field name escaping.
- Many bug fixes.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release v0.2.0, the project added Python 3 support, the ability to print the HTTP request as well as the response via --print and --verbose, support for Digest authentication, file upload support, improved syntax highlighting for JSON, support for field name escaping, and many bug fixes.

chunks.title: v0.1.6
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli

Tag: 0.1.6; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2015-02-01T14:11:16Z; created_at: 2012-03-04T12:47:09Z

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release v0.1.6, the project fixed an IOError in setup.py.

chunks.title: HTTPie 0.9.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 0.9.1
Tag: 0.9.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2015-02-07T16:08:43Z; created_at: 2015-02-07T16:04:13Z
Release notes:
- Added support for Requests transport adapter plugins to enable plugin-provided features such as [unix socket](https://github.com/msabramo/httpie-unixsocket) communication and [HTTP/2](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie-http2).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.1, the project added support for Requests transport adapter plugins to enable plugin-provided features such as unix socket communication and HTTP/2.

chunks.title: HTTPie 0.9.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 0.9.2
Tag: 0.9.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2015-02-24T06:55:41Z; created_at: 2015-02-24T06:49:24Z
Release notes:
- Fixed compatibility with Requests 2.5.1
- Changed the default JSON `Content-Type` to `application/json` as UTF-8
is the default JSON encoding
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.2, the project fixed compatibility with Requests 2.5.1 and changed the default JSON Content-Type to application/json as UTF-8 is the default JSON encoding.

chunks.title: HTTPie 0.9.3
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 0.9.3
Tag: 0.9.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2016-01-01T22:29:50Z; created_at: 2016-01-01T22:27:07Z
Release notes:
- Changed the default color `--style` from `solarized` to `monokai`
- Added basic Bash autocomplete support (need to be installed manually)
- Added request details to connection error messages
- Fixed `'requests.packages.urllib3' has no attribute 'disable_warnings'`
errors that occurred in some installations
- Fixed colors and formatting on Windows
- Fixed `--auth` prompt on Windows
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.3, the project changed the default color --style from solarized to monokai, added basic Bash autocomplete support, added request details to connection error messages, fixed 'requests.packages.urllib3' has no attribute 'disable_warnings' errors, fixed colors and formatting on Windows, and fixed --auth prompt on Windows.

chunks.title: HTTPie 0.9.4
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 0.9.4
Tag: 0.9.4; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2016-07-01T17:06:31Z; created_at: 2016-07-01T17:02:34Z
Release notes:
- Added `Content-Type` of files uploaded in `multipart/form-data` requests
- Added `--ssl=<PROTOCOL>` to specify the desired SSL/TLS protocol version
to use for HTTPS requests.
- Added JSON detection with `--json, -j` to work around incorrect
`Content-Type`
- Added `--all` to show intermediate responses such as redirects (with `--follow`)
- Added `--history-print, -P WHAT` to specify formatting of intermediate responses
- Added `--max-redirects=N` (default 30)
- Added `-A` as short name for `--auth-type`
- Added `-F` as short name for `--follow`
- Removed the `implicit_content_type` config option
(use `"default_options": ["--form"]` instead)
- Redirected `stdout` doesn't trigger an error anymore when `--output FILE`
is set
- Changed the default `--style` back to `solarized` for better support
of light and dark terminals
- Improved `--debug` output
- Fixed `--session` when used with `--download`
- Fixed `--download` to trim too long filenames before saving the file
- Fixed the handling of `Content-Type` with multiple `+subtype` parts
- Removed the XML formatter as the implementation suffered from multiple issues
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.4, the project added Content-Type of files uploaded in multipart/form-data requests, --ssl=<PROTOCOL> to specify the desired SSL/TLS protocol version, JSON detection with --json, -j, --all to show intermediate responses, --history-print, -P WHAT, --max-redirects=N, -A as short name for --auth-type, -F as short name for --follow, removed the implicit_content_type config option, changed the default --style back to solarized, improved --debug output, fixed --session when used with --download, fixed --download to trim too long filenames, fixed handling of Content-Type with multiple +subtype parts, and removed the XML formatter.

chunks.title: HTTPie 0.9.6
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 0.9.6
Tag: 0.9.6; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2016-08-13T21:04:38Z; created_at: 2016-08-13T21:01:05Z
Release notes:
- Added Python 3 as a dependency for Homebrew installations
to ensure some of the newer HTTP features work out of the box
for macOS users (starting with HTTPie 0.9.4.).
- Added the ability to unset a request header with `Header:`, and send an
empty value with `Header;`.
- Added `--default-scheme <URL_SCHEME>` to enable things like
`$ alias https='http --default-scheme=https`.
- Added `-I` as a shortcut for `--ignore-stdin`.
- Added fish shell completion (located in `extras/httpie-completion.fish`
in the Github repo).
- Updated `requests` to 2.10.0 so that SOCKS support can be added via
`pip install requests[socks]`.
- Changed the default JSON `Accept` header from `application/json`
to `application/json, */*`.
- Changed the pre-processing of request HTTP headers so that any leading
and trailing whitespace is removed.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.6, the project added Python 3 as a dependency for Homebrew installations, the ability to unset a request header with Header: and send an empty value with Header;, --default-scheme <URL_SCHEME>, -I as a shortcut for --ignore-stdin, fish shell completion, updated requests to 2.10.0 for SOCKS support, changed the default JSON Accept header to application/json, */*, and changed pre-processing of request HTTP headers to remove leading and trailing whitespace.

chunks.title: HTTPie 0.9.8
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 0.9.8
Tag: 0.9.8; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2016-12-08T04:28:24Z; created_at: 2016-12-08T04:22:20Z
Release notes:
- Extended auth plugin API.
- Added exit status code `7` for plugin errors.
- Added support for `curses`-less Python installations.
- Fixed `REQUEST_ITEM` arg incorrectly being reported as required.
- Improved `CTRL-C` interrupt handling.
- Added the standard exit status code `130` for keyboard interrupts.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.8, the project extended the auth plugin API, added exit status code 7 for plugin errors, added support for curses-less Python installations, fixed REQUEST_ITEM arg incorrectly being reported as required, improved CTRL-C interrupt handling, and added the standard exit status code 130 for keyboard interrupts.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 0.9.9, the project made only README changes.

chunks.title: HTTPie 1.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 1.0.0
Tag: 1.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2018-11-02T15:31:59Z; created_at: 2018-11-02T15:28:17Z
Release notes:
* Added ``--style=auto`` which follows the terminal ANSI color styles.
* Added support for selecting TLS 1.3 via ``--ssl=tls1.3``
(available once implemented in upstream libraries).
* Added ``true``/``false`` as valid values for ``--verify``
(in addition to ``yes``/``no``) and the boolean value is case-insensitive.
* Changed the default ``--style`` from ``solarized`` to ``auto`` (on Windows it stays ``fruity``).
* Fixed default headers being incorrectly case-sensitive.
* Removed Python 2.6 support.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 1.0.0, the project added --style=auto which follows the terminal ANSI color styles, support for selecting TLS 1.3 via --ssl=tls1.3, true/false as valid values for --verify, changed the default --style from solarized to auto (on Windows it stays fruity), fixed default headers being incorrectly case-sensitive, and removed Python 2.6 support.

chunks.title: HTTPie 1.0.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 1.0.2
Tag: 1.0.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2018-11-14T15:39:15Z; created_at: 2018-11-14T15:36:19Z
Release notes:

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 1.0.2, the project fixed tests for installation with pyOpenSSL.

chunks.title: HTTPie 1.0.3
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 1.0.3
Tag: 1.0.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2019-08-26T10:53:44Z; created_at: 2019-08-26T10:42:34Z
Release notes:
Fixed CVE-2019-10751 — the way the output filename is generated for `--download` requests without `--output` resulting in a redirect has been changed to only consider the initial URL as the base for the generated filename, and not the final one. This fixes a potential security issue under the following scenario:
1. A `--download` request with no explicit `--output` is made (e.g., `$ http -d  example.org/file.txt`), instructing HTTPie to [generate the output filename](https://httpie.org/doc#downloaded-filename) from the `Content-Disposition` response header, or from the URL if the header is not provided.
2. The server handling the request has been modified by an attacker and instead of the expected response the URL returns a redirect to another URL, e.g., `attacker.example.org/.bash_profile`, whose response does not provide  a `Content-Disposition` header (i.e., the base for the  generated filename becomes `.bash_profile` instead of `file.txt`).
3. Your current directory doesn’t already contain `.bash_profile` (i.e., no unique suffix is added to the generated filename).
4. You don’t notice the potentially unexpected output filename as reported by HTTPie in the console output (e.g., `Downloading 100.00 B to ".bash_profile"`).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 1.0.3, the project fixed CVE-2019-10751 by changing the way the output filename is generated for --download requests without --output resulting in a redirect to only consider the initial URL as the base for the generated filename, and not the final one.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.0.0
Tag: 2.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-01-12T10:11:10Z; created_at: 2020-01-12T10:06:43Z
Release notes:
* Removed Python 2.7 support (Python 3.6+ is now required).
* Added `--offline` to allow building an HTTP request and printing it but not actually sending it over the network.
* Replaced the old collect-all-then-process handling of HTTP communication with one-by-one processing of each HTTP request or response as they become available. This means that you can see headers immediately, see what is being sent even if the request fails, etc.
* Removed automatic config file creation to avoid concurrency issues.
* Removed the default 30-second connection `--timeout` limit.
* Removed Python’s default limit of 100 response headers.
* Added `--max-headers` to allow setting the max header limit.
* Added `--compres`s to allow request body compression.
* Added `--ignore-netrc` to allow bypassing credentials from `.netrc`.
* Added `https` alias command with `https://` as the default scheme.
* Added `$ALL_PROXY` documentation.
* Added type annotations throughout the codebase.
* Added `tests/` to the PyPi package for the convenience of downstream package maintainers.
* Fixed an error when `stdin` was a closed fd.
* Improved `--debug` output formatting.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.0.0, the project removed Python 2.7 support (Python 3.6+ is now required), added --offline to allow building an HTTP request and printing it but not actually sending it over the network, replaced the old collect-all-then-process handling of HTTP communication with one-by-one processing, removed automatic config file creation, removed the default 30-second connection --timeout limit, removed Python's default limit of 100 response headers, added --max-headers, added --compress, added --ignore-netrc, added https alias command, added $ALL_PROXY documentation, added type annotations, added tests/ to the PyPi package, fixed an error when stdin was a closed fd, and improved --debug output formatting.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.1.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.1.0
Tag: 2.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-04-18T11:08:29Z; created_at: 2020-04-18T10:54:40Z
Release notes:
* Added `--path-as-is` to bypass dot segment (`/../` or `/./`) URL squashing (#895).
* Changed the default `Accept` header value for JSON requests from `application/json, */*` to `application/json, */*;q=0.5` to clearly indicate preference (#488).
* Fixed `--form` file upload mixed with redirected `stdin` error handling (#840).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.1.0, the project added --path-as-is to bypass dot segment URL squashing, changed the default Accept header value for JSON requests to application/json, */*;q=0.5, and fixed --form file upload mixed with redirected stdin error handling.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.2.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.2.0
Tag: 2.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-06-18T20:27:25Z; created_at: 2020-06-18T20:20:12Z
Release notes:
* Added support for custom content types for uploaded files (#927, #668).
* Added support for `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (#920).
* Added support for `Set-Cookie`-triggered cookie expiration (#853).
* Added `--format-options` to allow disabling sorting, changing JSON indent size, etc. (#128)
* Added `--sorted` and `--unsorted` shortcuts for (un)setting all sorting-related `--format-options`. (#128)
* Added `--ciphers` to allow configuring OpenSSL ciphers (#870).
* Added `netrc` support for auth plugins. Enabled for `--auth-type=basic` and digest, 3rd parties may opt in (#718, #719, #852, #934).
* Fixed built-in plugins-related circular imports (#925).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.2.0, the project added support for custom content types for uploaded files, support for $XDG_CONFIG_HOME, support for Set-Cookie-triggered cookie expiration, --format-options, --sorted and --unsorted shortcuts, --ciphers, netrc support for auth plugins, and fixed built-in plugins-related circular imports.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.3.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.3.0
Tag: 2.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-10-25T20:52:58Z; created_at: 2020-10-25T19:39:01Z
Release notes:
*   Added support for streamed uploads ([#201](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/201)).
*   Added support for multipart upload streaming ([#684](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/684)).
*   Added support for body-from-file upload streaming (`http httpbin.org/post @file`).
*   Added `--chunked` to enable chunked transfer encoding ([#753](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/753)).
*   Added `--multipart` to allow `multipart/form-data` encoding for non-file `--form` requests as well.
*   Added support for preserving field order in multipart requests ([#903](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/903)).
*   Added `--boundary` to allow a custom boundary string for `multipart/form-data` requests.
*   Added support for combining cookies specified on the CLI and in a session file ([#932](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/932)).
*   Added out of the box SOCKS support with no extra installation ([#904](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/904)).
*   Added `--quiet, -q` flag to enforce silent behaviour.
*   Fixed the handling of invalid `expires` dates in `Set-Cookie` headers ([#963](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/963)).
*   Removed Tox testing entirely ([#943](https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/943)).
HTTPie /aitch-tee-tee-pie/ CLI is a user-friendly command-line HTTP client for the API era. It comes with JSON support, syntax highlighting, persistent sessions, wget-like downloads, plugins, and more.
Learn more: https://httpie.org
Install HTTPie: https://httpie.org/docs#installation
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.3.0, the project added support for streamed uploads, multipart upload streaming, body-from-file upload streaming, --chunked, --multipart, preserving field order in multipart requests, --boundary, combining cookies specified on the CLI and in a session file, out of the box SOCKS support, --quiet, -q flag, fixed handling of invalid expires dates in Set-Cookie headers, and removed Tox testing entirely.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.4.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.4.0
Tag: 2.4.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-02-06T10:23:44Z; created_at: 2021-02-06T10:17:24Z
Release notes:
HTTPie /aitch-tee-tee-pie/ CLI is a human-friendly CLI HTTP client for the API era.
The 2.4.0 release introduces the following improvements:
* Added support for `--session` cookie expiration based on `Set-Cookie: max-age=<n>`. (#1029)
* Show a `--check-status` warning with `--quiet` as well, not only when the output is redirected. (#1026)
* Fixed upload with `--session` (#1020).
* Fixed a missing blank line between request and response (#1006).
Join our Discord community: https://httpie.io/chat
Install HTTPie: https://httpie.io/docs#installation
Learn more: https://httpie.io
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.4.0, the project added support for --session cookie expiration based on Set-Cookie: max-age=<n>, showed a --check-status warning with --quiet as well, fixed upload with --session, and fixed a missing blank line between request and response.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.5.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.5.0
Tag: 2.5.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-09-06T18:29:23Z; created_at: 2021-09-06T18:23:14Z
Release notes:
Blog post: **[What’s new in HTTPie 2.5.0](https://httpie.io/blog/httpie-2.5.0)**
- Added `--raw` to allow specifying the raw request body without extra processing as
an alternative to `stdin`. ([#534](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/534))
- Added support for XML formatting. ([#1129](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1129))
- Added internal support for file-like object responses to improve adapter plugin support. ([#1094](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1094))
- Fixed `--continue --download` with a single byte to be downloaded left. ([#1032](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1032))
- Fixed `--verbose` HTTP 307 redirects with streamed request body. ([#1088](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1088))
- Fixed handling of session files with `Cookie:` followed by other headers. ([#1126](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1126))
---
→ Join our Discord community: https://httpie.io/chat
→ Install HTTPie: https://httpie.io/docs#installation
→ Learn more: https://httpie.io
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.5.0, the project added --raw to allow specifying the raw request body without extra processing, support for XML formatting, internal support for file-like object responses, fixed --continue --download with a single byte left, fixed --verbose HTTP 307 redirects with streamed request body, and fixed handling of session files with Cookie: followed by other headers.

chunks.title: HTTPie 2.6.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 2.6.0
Tag: 2.6.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-10-14T09:27:37Z; created_at: 2021-10-14T08:40:12Z
Release notes:
Blog post: **[What’s new in HTTPie 2.6.0](https://httpie.io/blog/httpie-2.6.0)**
- Added support for formatting & coloring of JSON bodies preceded by non-JSON data (e.g., an XXSI prefix). (#1130)
- Added charset auto-detection when `Content-Type` doesn’t include it. (#1110, #1168)
- Added `--response-charset` to allow overriding the response encoding for terminal display purposes. (#1168)
- Added `--response-mime` to allow overriding the response mime type for coloring and formatting for the terminal. (#1168)
- Added the ability to silence warnings through using `-q` or `--quiet twice` (e.g. `-qq`) (#1175)
- Added installed plugin list to `--debug` output. (#1165)
- Fixed duplicate keys preservation in JSON data. (#1163)
---
→ Join our Discord community: https://httpie.io/discord
→ Install HTTPie: https://httpie.io/docs#installation
→ Learn more: https://httpie.io
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 2.6.0, the project added support for formatting & coloring of JSON bodies preceded by non-JSON data, charset auto-detection, --response-charset, --response-mime, the ability to silence warnings through using -q or --quiet twice, added installed plugin list to --debug output, and fixed duplicate keys preservation in JSON data.

chunks.title: HTTPie 3.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 3.0.0
Tag: 3.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-01-21T17:43:17Z; created_at: 2022-01-21T17:34:38Z
Release notes:
✨ [What’s new in HTTPie for Terminal 3.0 →](https://httpie.io/blog/httpie-3.0.0)
🌲️ Nested JSON
🔌 Plugin manager
⏱️ Response metadata
🚀 Speed-ups
🎨 Improved UI/UX
✨ Other minor features
🪲 Bug fixes
- Dropped support for Python 3.6. ([#1177](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1177))
- Improved startup time by 40%. ([#1211](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1211))
- Added support for nested JSON syntax. ([#1169](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1169))
- Added `httpie plugins` interface for plugin management. ([#566](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/566))
- Added support for Bearer authentication via `--auth-type=bearer` ([#1215](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1215)).
- Added support for quick conversions of pasted URLs into HTTPie calls by adding a space after the protocol name (`$ https ://pie.dev` → `https://pie.dev`). ([#1195](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1195))
- Added support for _sending_ multiple HTTP header lines with the same name. ([#130](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/130))
- Added support for _receiving_ multiple HTTP headers lines with the same name. ([#1207](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1207))
- Added support for basic JSON types on `--form`/`--multipart` when using JSON only operators (`:=`/`:=@`). ([#1212](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1212))
- Added support for automatically enabling `--stream` when `Content-Type` is `text/event-stream`. ([#376](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/376))
- Added support for displaying the total elapsed time through `--meta`/`-vv` or `--print=m`. ([#243](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/243))
- Added new `pie-dark`/`pie-light` (and `pie`) styles that match with [HTTPie for Web and Desktop](https://httpie.io/product). ([#1237](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1237))
- Added support for better error handling on DNS failures. ([#1248](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1248))
- Added support for storing prompted passwords in the local sessions. ([#1098](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1098))
- Added warnings about the `--ignore-stdin`, when there is no incoming data from stdin. ([#1255](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1255))
- Fixed crashing due to broken plugins. ([#1204](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1204))
- Fixed auto addition of XML declaration to every formatted XML response. ([#1156](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1156))
- Fixed highlighting when `Content-Type` specifies `charset`. ([#1242](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1242))
- Fixed an unexpected crash when `--raw` is used with `--chunked`. ([#1253](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1253))
- Changed the default Windows theme from `fruity` to `auto`. ([#1266](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1266))
---
→ Join our Discord community: https://httpie.io/discord
→ Install HTTPie: https://httpie.io/docs/cli/installation
→ Learn more: https://httpie.io
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 3.0.0, the project dropped support for Python 3.6, improved startup time by 40%, added support for nested JSON syntax, added httpie plugins interface for plugin management, added support for Bearer authentication via --auth-type=bearer, added support for quick conversions of pasted URLs into HTTPie calls, added support for sending multiple HTTP header lines with the same name, added support for receiving multiple HTTP headers lines with the same name, added support for basic JSON types on --form/--multipart, added support for automatically enabling --stream when Content-Type is text/event-stream, added support for displaying the total elapsed time through --meta/-vv or --print=m, added new pie-dark/pie-light styles, added support for better error handling on DNS failures, added support for storing prompted passwords in local sessions, added warnings about --ignore-stdin when there is no incoming data from stdin, fixed crashing due to broken plugins, fixed auto addition of XML declaration to every formatted XML response, fixed highlighting when Content-Type specifies charset, fixed an unexpected crash when --raw is used with --chunked, and changed the default Windows theme from fruity to auto.

chunks.title: HTTPie 3.1.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 3.1.0
Tag: 3.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-03-07T22:53:25Z; created_at: 2022-03-07T22:50:09Z
Release notes:
- **SECURITY** Fixed the [vulnerability](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/security/advisories/GHSA-9w4w-cpc8-h2fq) that caused exposure of cookies on redirects to third party hosts. ([#1312](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1312))
- Fixed escaping of integer indexes with multiple backslashes in the nested JSON builder. ([#1285](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1285))
- Fixed displaying of status code without a status message on non-`auto` themes. ([#1300](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1300))
- Fixed redundant issuance of stdin detection warnings on some rare cases due to underlying implementation. ([#1303](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1303))
- Fixed double `--quiet` so that it will now suppress all python level warnings. ([#1271](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1271))
- Added support for specifying certificate private key passphrases through `--cert-key-pass` and prompts. ([#946](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/946))
- Added `httpie cli export-args` command for exposing the parser specification for the `http`/`https` commands. ([#1293](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1293))
- Improved regulation of top-level arrays. ([#1292](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/commit/225dccb2186f14f871695b6c4e0bfbcdb2e3aa28))
- Improved UI layout for standalone invocations. ([#1296](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1296))
---
→ Join our Discord community: https://httpie.io/discord
→ Install HTTPie: https://httpie.io/docs/cli/installation
→ Learn more: https://httpie.io
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 3.1.0, the project fixed a security vulnerability that caused exposure of cookies on redirects to third party hosts, fixed escaping of integer indexes with multiple backslashes in the nested JSON builder, fixed displaying of status code without a status message on non-auto themes, fixed redundant issuance of stdin detection warnings, fixed double --quiet to suppress all python level warnings, added support for specifying certificate private key passphrases through --cert-key-pass and prompts, added httpie cli export-args command, improved regulation of top-level arrays, and improved UI layout for standalone invocations.

chunks.title: HTTPie 3.2.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: httpie/cli
Release: HTTPie 3.2.0
Tag: 3.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-05-05T20:34:03Z; created_at: 2022-05-05T20:32:35Z
Release notes:
- Added a warning for notifying the user about the new updates. ([#1336](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1336))
- Added support for single binary executables. ([#1330](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1330))
- Added support for man pages (and auto generation of them from the parser declaration). ([#1317](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1317))
- Added `http --manual` for man pages & regular manual with pager. ([#1343](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1343))
- Added support for session persistence of repeated headers with the same name. ([#1335](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1335))
- Added support for sending `Secure` cookies to the `localhost` (and `.local` suffixed domains). ([#1308](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1308))
- Improved UI for the progress bars. ([#1324](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1324))
- Fixed redundant creation of `Content-Length` header on `OPTIONS` requests. ([#1310](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1310))
- Fixed blocking of warning thread on some use cases. ([#1349](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1349))
- Changed `httpie plugins` to the new `httpie cli` namespace as `httpie cli plugins` (`httpie plugins` continues to work as a hidden alias). ([#1320](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/issues/1320))
- Soft deprecated the `--history-print`. ([#1380](https://github.com/httpie/httpie/pull/1380))
---
→ Join our Discord community: https://httpie.io/discord
→ Install HTTPie: https://httpie.io/docs/cli/installation
→ Learn more: https://httpie.io
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the httpie/cli release HTTPie 3.2.0, the project added a warning for notifying the user about new updates, support for single binary executables, support for man pages and auto generation of them, http --manual for man pages & regular manual with pager, support for session persistence of repeated headers with the same name, support for sending Secure cookies to localhost and .local suffixed domains, improved UI for progress bars, fixed redundant creation of Content-Length header on OPTIONS requests, fixed blocking of warning thread on some use cases, changed httpie plugins to the new httpie cli namespace as httpie cli plugins, and soft deprecated --history-print.

questions.query_text: In the httpie/cli releases, which release adds support for nested JSON syntax and a plugin manager interface?

answer_support.answer_text: added support for nested JSON syntax, added httpie plugins interface for plugin management

questions.query_text: Across the httpie/cli releases, how does the SSL/TLS-related change in HTTPie 0.9.4 differ from the SSL-related change in 3.2.3?

options.text: 0.9.4 adds --ssl=<PROTOCOL> to specify the desired SSL/TLS protocol version, while 3.2.3 fixes SSL connections by pinning the requests version to 2.31.0.
0.9.4 removes SSL support, while 3.2.3 adds TLS 1.3 support.
Both releases only update the default --style option.

questions.expected_answer: 0.9.4 adds --ssl=<PROTOCOL> to specify the desired SSL/TLS protocol version, while 3.2.3 fixes SSL connections by pinning the requests version to 2.31.0.

answer_support.answer_text: Added --ssl=<PROTOCOL> to specify the desired SSL/TLS protocol version
Fix SSL connections by pinning the requests version to 2.31.0

questions.query_text: In the httpie/cli release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds support for nested JSON syntax and a plugin manager, (2) a release that adds --raw to allow specifying the raw request body without extra processing, (3) a release that adds support for streamed uploads and multipart upload streaming, (4) a release that adds --offline to allow building an HTTP request and printing it but not actually sending it over the network.

answer_support.answer_text: Added --offline to allow building an HTTP request and printing it but not actually sending it over the network.
Added support for streamed uploads, multipart upload streaming
Added --raw to allow specifying the raw request body without extra processing
Added support for nested JSON syntax, added httpie plugins interface for plugin management
